*Navamsa = Demands correction of 26 min 40 sec max.
*Dwadasamsa = Demands correction of 20 min max.
Note the huge difference of correction demands by the
first two and the last two methods. If the astrologer is forced
to depend on any of the last two (i.e. if the error in given BT
is big, and if the native is not sure about the accuracy of the
given BT) then the astrologer is sure to be in confusion, since
there could be several moments in between prior to the 20
min correction itself, where the Navamsa-Navamsa longitude
and Navamsa-Dwadasamsa longitude can conjunct and
provide a possible rhythmic combination. Thus the inherent
advice of this system is to "Note the BT accurately as far as
possible, and apply the least amount ofc orrection on given
BT, only to make it rhythmic with the position of Moon". Or
in other words "Do BT rectification, but don't try to do too
much correction; Dear astrologer, know your limitations and
